text.title: django-project-structure Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for the HackSoft-style Django layout.

rule.statement: Organise domain code under apps/<domain>/. One business domain per app. Apps live under a single apps/ namespace (`apps.users`, `apps.billing`, …).
rule.rationale: Domain-named folders let any reader find every file related to one concept in one place. Flat global folders (models/, views/, serializers/) force cross-folder hunts on every change.

rule.statement: Each app has exactly: models.py, services.py, selectors.py, apis.py, serializers.py, urls.py, constants.py, admin.py, and a tests/ subdirectory with test_models.py, test_services.py, test_selectors.py, test_apis.py.
rule.rationale: A canonical file list means a developer (or codegen agent) knows where to put a new function before they start writing it. selectors.py = reads, services.py = writes — the split is the architecture's core insight.

rule.statement: core/ provides shared infrastructure (BaseModel, exceptions, pagination, permissions). core/ MUST NOT import from apps/. Apps may import from core/.
rule.rationale: A directional dependency graph (apps → core) eliminates whole classes of circular ImportError. Reverse imports (core → apps) recreate them.

rule.statement: Settings + URL conf + ASGI/WSGI + Celery live in config/ (not in the project root and not in an app). Settings split into base.py + dev.py + staging.py + prod.py, each inheriting from base.
rule.rationale: Settings split keeps secrets out of dev defaults and makes env-specific overrides reviewable. Putting settings inside an app conflates configuration with domain code.

rule.statement: App folders: snake_case plural nouns (users, orders, user_profiles). Model classes: PascalCase singular (User, Order). Service functions: entity_action (user_create, order_process). Selector functions: entity_query (user_list, order_get_by_id). Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Choices classes: PascalCase (OrderStatus).
rule.rationale: A consistent naming convention makes the codebase grep-able: `grep -r "user_create"` finds every write to a User. Mixed conventions force every reader to translate.
